name: Tokyo Neighborhood Traffic Simulation
description: I was tasked with modeling changes to a neighborhood's road network using SUMO traffic simulation software for a studio with the municipal government of Chiyoda Ward, Tokyo. My team suggested a series of pedestrian upgrades and road diets; we needed to demonstrate how the proposed changes would impact the flow of vehicles and pedestrians. I created two models of the neighborhood's roadways: a base case, representing the network as it is now (extracted from Open Street Maps) and one edited to include our proposed changes. Trips were generated using kernel density estimation based on GPS data obtained from the University of Tokyo. By comparing overall travel times between the two scenarios, I found that the network changes would improve pedestrian flow at very little expense to vehicle traffic.

media.caption: A heatmap of vehicle flow created using the Python libraries Matplotlib and Contextly
The study area, fully modeled in SUMO
A heatmap of pedestrian flow created using the Python libraries Matplotlib and Contextly

name: Automated Bus Stop Inventory
description: Most transit agencies lack a comprehensive inventory of which amenities are present at their bus stops (shelters, seating, etc.) On top of this, the bus stop coordinates provided by transit agencies are often erroneous. I developed a system that uses reinforcement learning to actively seek out bus stops through street view imagery, compensating for unreliable coordinate data. Once it has located a stop, the system uses computer vision to automatically determine which amenities are present. It even locates multiple viewpoints of stops, enabling more accurate coordinates to be estimated through triangulation. Completed as part of my research position at the Center for Urban Analytics and Visualization (CURA) at Georgia Tech.

name: Graphics & Maps from Cooper Carry Internship
description: Maps I produced during my internship with the urban planning studio at Cooper Carry. I first arranged spatial data in ArcGIS, sometimes manually creating features based on parcel and roadway data. The resulting maps were refined in Adobe Illustrator and InDesign to create aesthetically pleasing graphics that can be understood by a non-technical audience. Each of these graphics were sent to clients as part of final deliverables.

media.caption: Used to provide site context for a public client on the Georgia coast. Data from FEMA.
Map made for a private developer in Texas. Data derived from OpenStreetMap and municipal sources.

name: ULI Hines Contest Submission (Writing Sample)
description: Selections from my team's entry for the 2024 ULI Hines competition. As the team's city planner, I conducted site analysis using ArcGIS, wrote the narrative, and assisted with graphics using Illustrator and InDesign.

name: Thesis: Rail Transit Resilience Strategies
description: My master's thesis examines how rail transit systems around the world are preparing for more frequent extreme precipitation events and flooding, and what constitutes an effective resilience approach. I conducted interviews with personnel from five different transit agencies, examining how approaches differ and the factors that have enabled the most effective responses.

media.caption: A flooded subway station in NYC

name: Rainwater Runoff Study
description: I used ArcGIS to determine how different development types (suburban vs. urban) in the Atlanta area impact rainwater runoff. I first determined groundcover type (foliage vs. impermeable surfaces) per parcel using machine learning tools applied to satellite imagery. Given that plants absorb infrared at a higher rate than their surroundings, the infrared band of satellite imagery can be used to determine which pixels contain foliage. The groundcover ratio was then used to estimate surface runoff.

name: Emissions Modeling
description: I used a pollutant dispersion model provided by the EPA to gauge the area impacted by a coal-burning powerplant. The results were cross-referenced with census data to conduct an analysis on which demographics were most impacted by the fallout, such those designated as disadvantaged.
